title: brachyglottis × spedenii
intro: Brachyglottis × spedenii, also known as the cabbage tree or Ti Kouka, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats. Additionally, the plant has been used to support digestive health and to help reduce fever, making it a valuable remedy in traditional New Zealand herbalism.
